creator: linas@in.spb.ru
text:
Hi!

While copying file from remote server via scp copying process stalled in suspfs state. From this moment any process trying to access same fs became stalled in the same suspfs state. Processes won't be killed even with kill -9. Problem been resolved with reboot and can't be reproduced.

We can't provide syslog logs since one log been configured on affected fs and syslogd has been trapped in the same suspfs state.

Uname output:

FreeBSD dbmain 10.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 10.1-RELEASE #0 r274401: Tue Nov 11 21:02:49 UTC 2014     root@releng1.nyi.freebsd.org:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64

Affected fs is /1 mount point

% mount
/dev/da0s1a on / (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
devfs on /dev (devfs, local, multilabel)
/dev/da0s1d on /tmp (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
/dev/da0s1e on /var (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
/dev/da0s1f on /usr (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
/dev/da0s1g on /1 (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
/dev/da1s1a on /data (ufs, local, journaled soft-updates)
